D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Photopic & Scotopic Sensitivity

dim the lights and watch the eye's peak sensitivity shift blueward — the Purkinje effect

Cone-driven daylight vision (photopic V(λ)) peaks at 555 nm; rod-driven night vision (scotopic V′(λ)) peaks at 507 nm. Between them the eye cross-fades — and reds fade faster than blues.

Slide adaptation from bright daylight to deep night. The blended curve and the two swatches show how a red and a blue patch swap apparent brightness — the Purkinje shift.
